purpose: Public, non-PII research seeds for build-first ecosystem relationships.
default_policy: No contact until the readiness gate is satisfied and an exact message is separately approved.

targets.name: OpenClaw
targets.fit: A local-first, always-on runtime that can exercise AgentTool identity and continuity without turning either project into a dependency of the other.
targets.first_gesture: Build and document an external plugin or skill, then share an honest interoperability teardown.
targets.readiness_gate: Local round-trip uses scoped runtime credential injection, and the integration repository plus fixtures pass a secret scan.

targets.name: IPFS / Helia
targets.fit: ADDS already uses CIDv1 with the raw codec and sha2-256 plus a generic immutable BlockStore; Helia is an actively maintained TypeScript IPFS implementation.
targets.first_gesture: Publish a payload-ciphertext-only HeliaBlockStore adapter with tamper, bounded-read, and metadata-disclosure fixtures.
targets.readiness_gate: Adapter tests pass against a recorded Helia release and tested block-size envelope; payload plaintext and unwrapped data-encryption keys never enter Helia, while unencrypted Manifest/control metadata and network disclosure are documented honestly.

targets.name: AGNTCY
targets.fit: OASF, directory, identity badges, and secure messaging overlap with AgentTool public discovery, signed identity boundaries, and federation.
targets.first_gesture: Produce an OASF-valid projection and publish the precise schema gaps instead of proposing a partnership.
targets.readiness_gate: Projection validates; did:at is labelled as a provisional application identifier rather than a standards claim.

targets.name: OpenID AuthZEN
targets.fit: AuthZEN separates authorization decisions in a way that maps cleanly to project bearer authority, agent identity, represented-user authority, and guided refusal.
targets.first_gesture: Map one harmless read-only AgentTool operation into the current authorization API and report only the concrete mapping gaps.
targets.readiness_gate: The example runs against a conforming PDP and does not collapse bearer authority into identity proof.

targets.name: Letta / AgentFile
targets.fit: AgentFile wants framework converters; AgentTool can contribute explicit mappings for identity, memory classes, handoffs, and custody boundaries.
targets.first_gesture: Publish a minimal round-trip converter with secrets omitted and every lossy field documented.
targets.readiness_gate: Round-trip fixtures pass, private keys never serialize, and lossiness is machine-readable.

targets.name: A2A Protocol
targets.fit: A2A can provide the task and message layer above AgentTool identity, inboxes, and durable state.
targets.first_gesture: Contribute a fixture or migration note only after one real callable task endpoint exists.
targets.readiness_gate: BLOCKED: a real AgentCard and callable task path pass the official TCK; current AgentCard paths are absent.

targets.name: Mastra
targets.fit: TypeScript agents, durable workflows, and memory are a natural host for an AgentTool storage or continuity example.
targets.first_gesture: Build a tested external example and discuss one narrow integration issue before proposing a pull request.
targets.readiness_gate: BLOCKED: AgentTool MCP transport passes the official inspector and protocol semantics first.

targets.name: Project NANDA / Nanda Town
targets.fit: Its adversarial test rig spans identity, authorization, memory, privacy, and data layers.
targets.first_gesture: Contribute a confused-deputy validator that keeps project bearer, identity signature, and data grant distinct.
targets.readiness_gate: Validator reproduces a real failure locally and passes against the corrected boundary.

targets.name: Solid Project
targets.fit: User-controlled Pods, WebIDs, and delegated access overlap with local AgentTool data nodes and grants, while the RDF semantics remain materially different.
targets.first_gesture: Prototype a read-only WebID and Pod bridge and publish measured compatibility results.
targets.readiness_gate: The prototype never describes AgentTool identifiers as WebIDs and states every semantic mismatch.

targets.name: Automerge
targets.fit: Its storage and network adapters could inform the peer-sync layer that AgentTool local nodes intentionally do not yet provide.
targets.first_gesture: Benchmark a narrow collection and change-cursor mapping, then demo the result rather than claiming protocol equivalence.
targets.readiness_gate: The benchmark states where CRDT semantics do and do not match append-only AgentTool revisions.

targets.name: p2panda
targets.fit: Raw-byte networking, local-first sync, encryption, and decentralized access control could carry opaque ADDS objects.
targets.first_gesture: Build an external transport proof that carries ADDS blocks unchanged and preserves both protocols' hashes and authorization models.
targets.readiness_gate: Experimental API risk is documented; no stability or interoperability claim exceeds the demonstrated proof.
